main_functions.description: Allows user to create an account using email and password with validation and business rules.

sub_functions.description: Validates email format and uniqueness during registration.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that email must contain '@' and a domain, and that invalid formats are rejected with meaningful inline error messages.
Verify that duplicate email addresses are rejected with a meaningful inline error message.

sub_functions.description: Validates password against all defined rules including length, character requirements, and restrictions.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that password length is between 8 and 12 characters inclusive, and that invalid lengths are rejected with meaningful inline error messages.
Verify that password contains at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one digit, and one special character from the allowed set (e.g., @, #, $, %, ^, &, *).
Verify that consecutive spaces in the password are forbidden.
Verify that leading and trailing spaces in the password are not trimmed and are preserved as entered.
Verify that password does not contain the user's email prefix (case-sensitive comparison as per assumption).

sub_functions.description: Verifies that meaningful inline error messages are displayed for duplicate email, invalid email format, and invalid password length.

test_categories.category_name: Error Message Content for Duplicate Email
test_categories.test_intent: Verify that a meaningful inline error message is displayed when a duplicate email is entered.

test_categories.category_name: Error Message Content for Invalid Email Format
test_categories.test_intent: Verify that a meaningful inline error message is displayed when an invalid email format is entered.

test_categories.category_name: Error Message Content for Invalid Password Length
test_categories.test_intent: Verify that a meaningful inline error message is displayed when password length is outside 8-12 characters.

main_functions.description: Handles sending and resending of verification link, and verification process before first login.

sub_functions.description: System sends a unique verification link via email immediately after registration.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that a unique verification link is generated and sent via email immediately after successful registration.
Verify that the system handles email sending service failures gracefully, e.g., by logging the error and notifying the user appropriately.

sub_functions.description: Verification link is valid for 24 hours.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that the verification link expires after 24 hours and cannot be used for verification after expiration.
Verify that when a user clicks an expired verification link, the system displays an appropriate message and allows the user to request a new verification link or re-register.

sub_functions.description: User can request to resend the verification link after a 60-second cooldown.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that the resend request is rejected if attempted within 60 seconds of the previous request, and allowed after the cooldown period.

Verify that the rate-limiting mechanism handles multiple rapid requests (e.g., 10 requests within 1 second) and concurrent requests (e.g., two simultaneous requests) correctly, enforcing the 60-second cooldown.

sub_functions.description: User must verify email before first login.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that a user cannot log in without having verified their email address.

sub_functions.description: Validates the verification link token when user clicks the link.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that the system validates the verification token and rejects invalid, tampered, or malformed tokens with appropriate error handling.
Verify that the system rejects invalid, malformed, or tampered verification tokens and displays an appropriate error message.

main_functions.description: Handles user login after email verification.

sub_functions.description: Allows a user with a verified email to log in successfully.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that a user with a verified email can log in with correct credentials.

main_functions.description: Covers system failures, invalid tokens, expired links, and other edge cases not covered by normal flows.

sub_functions.description: Validates that only the specified special characters are accepted in passwords.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that passwords containing special characters from the allowed set (e.g., @, #, $, %, ^, &, *) are accepted.
Verify that passwords containing special characters not in the allowed set are rejected or handled as per system behavior.

sub_functions.description: Handles the open question of whether email prefix comparison for password restriction is case-sensitive.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that the password restriction for containing email prefix is case-sensitive (e.g., 'John' in password is allowed if email prefix is 'john').
Verify that the password restriction for containing email prefix is case-insensitive (e.g., 'John' in password is rejected if email prefix is 'john').
